questions.q: In Hindu mythology, which celestial body is the deity Chandra identified with?
questions.explanation: Chandra is the moon god of Hindu tradition, presiding over the moon in the night sky.

questions.q: The moon god Chandra is identified with which sacred substance of the Vedas?
questions.explanation: Chandra is identified with Soma, the sacred draught celebrated in the Vedas and associated with immortality.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, what animals draw the chariot of the moon god Chandra across the night sky?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Chandra riding a chariot drawn by antelopes across the night sky.

questions.q: Whose twenty-seven daughters did the moon god Chandra marry, according to the Puranas?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that Chandra wed the twenty-seven daughters of Daksha, who personify the lunar mansions.

questions.q: The twenty-seven wives of the moon god Chandra represent which group of celestial entities?
questions.explanation: The twenty-seven daughters of Daksha whom Chandra married personify the nakshatras, the lunar mansions.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, why was the moon god Chandra cursed to wane?
options: For neglecting all his wives but Rohini
questions.explanation: Chandra was cursed to wane because he favored Rohini and neglected his other twenty-six wives.

questions.q: Which of his many wives was the favorite of the moon god Chandra, leading to his curse?
questions.explanation: Chandra favored Rohini above his other wives, which provoked the curse that he would wane.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, what monthly phenomenon of the moon is explained by the curse upon Chandra?
options: The waxing and waning of the moon
questions.explanation: The curse upon Chandra is said to explain the moon's monthly cycle of waxing and waning.

questions.q: In the quiz deck of Indian mythology, the moon god Chandra is classified as which type of subject?
questions.explanation: Chandra is presented as a character, the personified moon god, in the Indian mythology collection.

questions.q: Among the Navagraha, the nine celestial influencers, which one is the moon god Chandra?
questions.explanation: Chandra represents the moon among the Navagraha, the nine planetary deities of Hindu astrology.

questions.q: In Hindu cosmology, Chandra is counted among which group of nine celestial deities?
questions.explanation: Chandra is one of the Navagraha, the nine planetary deities propitiated together in temples.

questions.q: Among the Navagraha, which two luminaries are Surya and Chandra respectively?
questions.explanation: In the Navagraha, Surya is the sun and Chandra is the moon.

questions.q: Which sacred drink of the Vedic yajna, drawn from a pressed plant, shares its name and identity with the moon god Chandra?
questions.explanation: Soma, the ritual draught pressed and offered in the Vedic sacrifice, is identified with the moon god Chandra.

questions.q: The lunar mansions whom Chandra married are the daughters of Daksha. Of what is Daksha a son or descendant in the Puranas?
options: A progenitor (Prajapati) in the line of creation
questions.explanation: Daksha is a Prajapati, a progenitor figure in Puranic genealogy, whose twenty-seven daughters Chandra married.

questions.q: In Hindu iconography of the planetary deities, what color is the moon god Chandra typically described as?
questions.explanation: Chandra, as the moon, is typically depicted with a pale, fair, or white complexion.

questions.q: The crescent of the moon, associated with Chandra, is worn as an ornament by which great deity of the Trimurti?
questions.explanation: Shiva wears the crescent moon in his matted locks, an emblem closely linked to Chandra.

questions.q: The wife Rohini, favored by Chandra, lends her name to which kind of celestial marker in Hindu astronomy?
questions.explanation: Rohini is one of the nakshatras, the lunar mansions personified as Chandra's wives.

questions.q: In Sanskrit, the name 'Chandra' most directly conveys which quality of the moon?
questions.explanation: The name Chandra carries the sense of the shining, bright, or luminous one, fitting the moon.

questions.q: Soma, identified with Chandra, is praised in the Vedas chiefly as what?
options: A sacred ritual draught and deity
questions.explanation: In the Vedas, Soma is exalted as a sacred ritual draught and deity, later identified with the moon Chandra.

questions.q: Which deity, mounted on a buffalo, is sometimes contrasted with Chandra as a lord of the night sky's opposite domain of death?
questions.explanation: Yama, lord of death mounted on a buffalo, is a distinct deity, whereas Chandra presides over the moon.

questions.q: The phases by which the moon waxes and wanes divide the lunar month into two halves. What is the bright, waxing half called?
questions.explanation: The bright waxing fortnight of the moon, governed by Chandra, is called Shukla Paksha.

questions.q: The night of the full moon, when Chandra shines at his fullest, is known in Sanskrit by which name?
questions.explanation: Purnima is the full-moon night, when Chandra appears wholly waxed and brightest.

questions.q: In Puranic genealogy, the moon god Chandra is regarded as the progenitor of which great royal dynasty?
questions.explanation: Chandra is held to be the founder of the Chandravamsha, the Lunar dynasty, in Puranic accounts.

questions.q: According to Puranic tradition, who pronounced the curse that caused the moon god Chandra to wane?
questions.explanation: Daksha, father of Chandra's wives, cursed him to wane for neglecting all but Rohini.

questions.q: In the Puranas, which planetary deity is traditionally regarded as a son born of Chandra?
questions.explanation: Budha, identified with the planet Mercury, is traditionally counted as a son of Chandra in the Puranas.

questions.q: After Daksha's curse, the relenting of the moon god Chandra's fate left the moon to do what, rather than vanish permanently?
options: Wax and wane in a recurring cycle
questions.explanation: The curse was softened so that Chandra would wane and wax in a recurring cycle rather than disappear forever.

questions.q: In the Puranic account of the churning of the ocean of milk, the moon (associated with Chandra) is named among which category of emergent items?
options: The mountains used as the rod
The treasures that arose from the churning
questions.explanation: Several Puranic lists count the moon among the treasures that arose from the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: Which weekday in Hindu tradition is named for and presided over by the moon god Chandra?
questions.explanation: Monday, called Somavara after Soma, is the weekday associated with the moon god Chandra.

questions.q: The dark, moonless night of the lunar month, when Chandra is wholly waned, is known by which Sanskrit name?
questions.explanation: Amavasya is the new-moon night, when Chandra has fully waned and the moon is unseen.

questions.q: Distinct from the moon god Chandra, which separate Vedic deity drives a chariot drawn by seven horses across the day sky?
questions.explanation: Surya, the sun god, rides a chariot of seven horses by day, whereas Chandra rides antelopes by night.
